description_long: This town hall is a large, multi-winged timber-framed building with a complex, steeply pitched green-tiled roof. The structure features prominent gabled sections and overhanging upper stories supported by wooden posts, evoking a medieval or Tudor architectural style. The facade is richly detailed with exposed wooden beams, large window panels, and decorative cross-bracing. The building is elevated above the ground on stilts, suggesting a historical adaptation to flooding or uneven terrain. Multiple entryways are visible, each framed by timber and set beneath the projecting upper floors. The overall impression is of a grand, historic civic building designed to serve as a focal point for a town or city.
description: A grand, timber-framed town hall with steep green roofs, gabled wings, and decorative wooden facades, elevated on stilts.
description_view_0: Oblique view showing the building's multiple gabled wings, overhanging upper stories, and the stilted base.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the symmetrical arrangement of the main entrance and the central gabled section.
description_view_2: Side view emphasizing the depth of the structure, the repeating gabled sections, and the timber framework.
description_view_3: Rear oblique view showing the back and side gables, as well as the stilted supports and window arrangements.
attribute_facade_ornamentation: Exposed timber framing, decorative cross-bracing, and large window panels with wooden mullions.
attribute_entrance_design: Multiple main entryways with wooden double doors, framed by timber posts and overhanging upper stories, no visible steps or portico.
